description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a bright yellow fuselage and dual wings stacked vertically, supported by brown struts. The airplane features a classic open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing, with a visible propeller mounted at the nose. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all in matching yellow with accent lines. The landing gear consists of two prominent floats, indicating this is a seaplane variant designed for water landings. The airplane is displayed on a dark, octagonal platform with yellow and white runway markings, enhancing its presentation. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, with a charming, stylized appearance.
description: A yellow vintage biplane seaplane with dual wings, open cockpit, and float landing gear, displayed on a marked platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, central propeller, and both floats clearly, with the cockpit partially visible behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the open cockpit, the length of the fuselage, and the float landing gear beneath the wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, including the single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, as well as the stacked wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit opening, the side profile of the floats, and the tail's roundel marking.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Float landing gear with two main floats mounted beneath the lower wing; fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat with no visible canopy.
